title: Extract errorMessage utility from 8 inline copies in GUI
analysis: The pattern `err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)` is repeated 8 times: 7 in App.tsx (lines 66, 73, 159, 171, 288, 303, 317) and 1 in Editor.tsx (line 115). All instances safely extract a string message from an `unknown` caught error. The existing `gui/src/utils/errorClassifier.ts` already exports `classifyError(message: string)` and has a comprehensive test file at `gui/src/__tests__/errorClassifier.test.ts`. Adding `errorMessage(err: unknown): string` to the same module is the natural choice — it's a closely related error-handling utility and avoids creating a new file. The refactoring is purely mechanical: export the function, import it in both consumer files, and replace the inline ternaries with `errorMessage(err)`. No existing tests mock or assert on the inline pattern directly, so no test modifications are needed beyond adding tests for the new utility itself.

steps.description: Add failing tests for `errorMessage` in errorClassifier.test.ts: (1) Error instance returns .message, (2) string input returns itself via String(), (3) number/object/null/undefined coerced via String(), (4) Error subclass returns .message
Add `export function errorMessage(err: unknown): string { return err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err); }` to gui/src/utils/errorClassifier.ts
Replace 7 inline `err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)` occurrences in App.tsx with `errorMessage(err)` and add the import from '../utils/errorClassifier'
Replace 1 inline `err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)` occurrence in Editor.tsx (line 115) with `errorMessage(err)` and add the import from '../utils/errorClassifier'
Run full GUI test suite (`npx vitest run` in gui/) to verify no regressions — all existing App.test.tsx and Editor.test.tsx tests must still pass

design_decisions.decision: Add errorMessage to existing errorClassifier.ts rather than creating a new errors.ts file
design_decisions.rationale: errorClassifier.ts is already the error-utility module in gui/src/utils/ and has an established test file. Adding a closely related function here avoids file proliferation and keeps error utilities co-located. The task description itself suggests this as the primary option.
Matches the existing inline pattern exactly. The `unknown` type is correct because all 8 call sites receive caught exceptions which are `unknown` in TypeScript strict mode.

design_decisions.decision: Keep the implementation as a simple one-liner ternary, not a multi-branch handler
design_decisions.rationale: The goal is DRY extraction, not behavior change. The existing pattern is well-understood and correct. Over-engineering (e.g., handling nested causes, aggregating errors) would change semantics and risk regressions.

design_decisions.decision: No changes to existing App.test.tsx or Editor.test.tsx test files
design_decisions.rationale: Grep confirmed no existing tests mock or assert on the inline ternary pattern. The refactoring is purely internal — external behavior (toast messages, error callbacks) is unchanged, so existing tests validate the refactoring implicitly.

reuse.what: errorClassifier.ts module and its test file
reuse.how: Add the new errorMessage function to the existing module and extend the existing test describe block rather than creating new files
Follow the same describe/it structure and import style already established in the file
